problem_diagnosis.name: Recommendation
problem_diagnosis.description:
You are an expert ML engineer tasked with designing and improving *diagnosis tools* for a sequential recommendation model.

Goal:
- Focus on build probes and metrics that analyze how the recommender behaves (e.g., embedding health, sequence sensitivity, popularity/price/genre biases).
- Output a stable, machine-readable MODEL_DIAGNOSIS structure.

Hardware / Runtime Requirements:
- The main recommender runs on CPU. Your probes must be lightweight.

Available Objects:
- Access to the trained model (embeddings, output logits).
- `self.meta_data`: Item information (title, categories, etc.).
- `self.review_data`: User reviews with ratings and text.
- `self.user_train`: User histories.

Design Requirements for Diagnosis:
- Define clear, interpretable metrics (embedding health, margin quality, etc.).
- Implement each metric as a small, testable function.

Acceptance Checklist:
- MODEL_DIAGNOSIS is a single, well-defined JSON-like dict.
- Metrics can be computed without exhausting CPU memory.

initial_idea_diagnosis.title: Model Diagnosis Tools
initial_idea_diagnosis.content: Improve the model diagnosis tool through mathematical analysis, code analysis, and research paper analysis
